content.Text: [Sun 2026-04-19 13:43 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at docs/graph-advisor.html for GraphVisual. This is a "Smart Graph Advisor" - users input a graph and it automatically runs multiple analyses and generates a comprehensive report with recommendations. ## What it does: 1. User inputs a graph (adjacency list text, or picks from 8 presets) 2. Clicks "Analyze" and the tool runs ALL of these checks automatically: - Basic stats (nodes, edges, density, avg degree) - Connectivity (connected? components count) - Bipartiteness check (BFS 2-coloring) - Cycle detection (DFS) - Tree detection - Planarity heuristic (edge count vs 3n-6) - Chromatic number estimate (greedy coloring) - Degree distribution analysis (power-law check) - Hub detection (nodes with degree > 2*avg) - Bridge detection - Isolated node detection 3. Displays results as a visual "report card" with: - Overall graph health score (0-100) - Category cards for each analysis with pass/warn/info badges - A "Recommended Tools" section that suggests which GraphVisual tools to use based on findings (e.g., if bipartite → suggest Matching tool; if has bridges → suggest Bridges tool; if dense → suggest Coloring) - Proactive insights ("Your graph has 3 hub nodes that control 67% of connections — consider resilience analysis") ## Styling: - Use `` for base styles - Follow the same dark theme pattern as other tools (use CSS variables: --bg, --surface, --border, --text, --text-muted, --accent, --accent-hover, --red, --orange, --green) - Layout: left panel (300px) for input controls, right area for report - Include the standard sidebar nav (copy from other pages, add this tool as "🧠 Graph Advisor" link) ## Sidebar nav links to include (copy exactly from other files like anomaly.html): Include ALL existing tool links in the sidebar plus add this new one: ```html 🧠Graph Advisor ``` Add it in the Tools section after "Max Matching". ## 8 Presets: 1. Simple Path (1-2-3-4-5) 2. Complete K5 3. Petersen Graph 4. Binary Tree (depth 4) 5. Bipartite K3,3 6. Star Graph (hub + 8 leaves) 7. Grid 4x4 8. Random Erdos-Renyi (20 nodes, p=0.15) ## Input format: Adjacency list, one edge per line: "A B" or "A-B" or "A,B" ## Technical: - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies - Canvas for a small graph visualization of the input - All computation in JS - ~800-1200 lines total - Make it look polished and professional ## Also: Update docs/index.html to add the sidebar link for Graph Advisor after the Max Matching link: ```html 🧠Graph Advisor ``` Add it right after the matching.html link, before the Architecture section. After creating the files, verify with: `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const html=fs.readFileSync('docs/graph-advisor.html','utf8'); console.log('Lines:', html.split('\\n').length); console.log('Has canvas:', html.includes('canvas')); console.log('Has presets:', html.includes('Petersen'))"`
